executive_summary.state_snapshot: Análisis completo: 18 requisitos funcionales normalizados en Gherkin, 4 áreas de testing identificadas (Registro, Listado, Navegación, API), 12 gaps críticos documentados
critical_findings: 🔴 CRITICAL: No hay validación de email más allá de HTML5 (impacta coverage de Registration)
🔴 CRITICAL: No hay rate limiting implementado (impacta load testing y security suite)
🟡 HIGH: Datos en memoria se pierden con el servidor (no hay persistencia - impacta data integrity tests)
🟡 HIGH: No hay autenticación/autorización (impacta security testing)
🟡 HIGH: No hay manejo de errores específico para malformed requests (impacta API contract testing)
🟡 MEDIUM: No hay límites de longitud de campos (impacta boundary value testing)
🟡 MEDIUM: No hay sanitización XSS visible en backend (impacta security tests)
🟢 LOW: Validación de teléfono solo HTML5 browser-side (verificar backend acceptance)
executive_summary.recommendation: Planner debe diseñar suites considerando estos gaps como bloqueadores o decision_points. Se recomienda feedback con dev team sobre persistencia y rate limiting antes de planificar load tests.

added: User Registration Flow: 5 requisitos (Register new user, Validate required fields, Accept optional phone, Show success message, Clear form on success)
User Listing Flow: 4 requisitos (Display empty state, Fetch users from API, Render user table, Show all user fields)
User Management Flow: 5 requisitos (Open edit modal, Update user data, Validate on update, Delete user with confirmation, Reload table after changes)
Navigation Flow: 2 requisitos (Navigate to registration page, Navigate to users page)
API Contract Tests: 2 requisitos (API responses with correct HTTP status codes, API error messages for validation failures)
delta_changes.rationale: Extracción de requisitos completada del análisis de código fuente (server.js, HTML/JS). Todos los requisitos normalizados a Gherkin con trazabilidad verificada a implementación. API endpoints documentados con payloads y respuestas esperadas.

requirements_normalized.title: Register new user with valid data
requirements_normalized.gherkin:
Given user is on registration page
When user fills name='{name}', email='{email}', phone='{phone}'
And user clicks Registrarse button
Then API receives POST /api/register with JSON payload
And user receives success message 'Registro exitoso. ¡Gracias!'
And form is cleared for next registration
acceptance_criteria: name field is required (HTML5 required attribute)
email field is required (HTML5 required attribute)
phone field is optional
POST request body must include: {name, email, phone}
Response status: 200
Response includes: {success: true, message: '...', user: {...}}

requirements_normalized.title: Validate required fields on registration
requirements_normalized.gherkin:
Given user is on registration page
When user submits form without '{missing_field}'
Then form HTML5 validation prevents submission
Or API responds with 400 error
acceptance_criteria: Browser-side validation with HTML5 required attribute
If name or email missing, API returns 400: {error: 'Name and email are required'}
If both fields missing, API response prevents invalid user creation

requirements_normalized.title: Accept optional phone number
requirements_normalized.gherkin:
Given registration form with phone field
When user submits form with phone value
And when user submits form without phone value
Then both registrations succeed
And phone is stored as-is (no formatting)
acceptance_criteria: phone field is optional (no HTML5 required attribute)
API accepts phone as string or null/empty
User object stores phone exactly as submitted

requirements_normalized.title: Display success message on registration
requirements_normalized.gherkin:
Given user submits valid registration
When API responds 200 with success
Then UI shows message element with class 'message success'
And message text is 'Registro exitoso. ¡Gracias!'
And message auto-clears after 3000ms
acceptance_criteria: Success message displays for 3 seconds
Message has CSS class 'message success' for styling
Message text matches exactly: 'Registro exitoso. ¡Gracias!'
After 3s, message clears automatically

requirements_normalized.title: Clear registration form after successful submission
requirements_normalized.gherkin:
Given user submits valid registration
When API responds 200
Then all form fields are cleared (name, email, phone empty)
And user can submit another registration without residual data
acceptance_criteria: form.reset() called after success
All input fields in form are empty after reset
No data from previous registration persists

requirements_normalized.title: Display empty state when no users registered
requirements_normalized.gherkin:
Given no users exist in the system
When user navigates to /users page
And page loads users via GET /api/users
Then no users table is visible
And 'No hay usuarios registrados' message displays
acceptance_criteria: Table element is hidden when users.length === 0
Message element #noUsers is visible
Message text: 'No hay usuarios registrados'

requirements_normalized.title: Fetch and display registered users
requirements_normalized.gherkin:
Given users exist in the system (via prior registrations)
When user navigates to /users page
Then page calls GET /api/users
And API returns array of user objects
And each user row displays: ID, Name, Email, Phone, RegistrationDate, Actions
acceptance_criteria: API endpoint /api/users returns JSON array
Each user object includes: {id, name, email, phone, registeredAt}
Table displays user.name in column 'Nombre'
Table displays user.email in column 'Email'
Table displays user.phone in column 'Teléfono'
Table displays user.registeredAt in column 'Fecha de Registro'

requirements_normalized.title: Display action buttons for each user
requirements_normalized.gherkin:
Given users table is displayed
When page renders user rows
Then each row includes 'Editar' and 'Eliminar' buttons
And buttons are data-bound to user.id
acceptance_criteria: Each user row has 'Editar' button with class 'btn-edit'
Each user row has 'Eliminar' button with class 'btn-delete'
Buttons have data-id attribute matching user.id

requirements_normalized.title: Open edit modal and load user data
requirements_normalized.gherkin:
Given user table is displayed
When user clicks 'Editar' button for a user
Then page calls GET /api/users/{id}
And modal appears with fields pre-filled: name, email, phone
And user can edit these fields
acceptance_criteria: GET /api/users/{id} returns single user object
Modal #editModal appears with display='block'
Form fields are pre-populated with user data
User can modify field values in modal

requirements_normalized.title: Update user data via edit form
requirements_normalized.gherkin:
Given edit modal is open with user data
When user modifies name, email, or phone
And user clicks 'Guardar' button
Then page sends PUT /api/users/{id} with updated data
And API validates name and email are not empty
And user record is updated
And modal closes
And table reloads with updated data
acceptance_criteria: PUT request body: {name, email, phone}
API validates: name and email are required
Response status 200: {success: true, message: '...', user: {...}}
Response status 400 if name or email missing
Modal closes after successful update
Table calls loadUsers() to refresh display

requirements_normalized.title: Delete user with confirmation
requirements_normalized.gherkin:
Given user table is displayed
When user clicks 'Eliminar' button
Then browser shows confirmation dialog: '¿Está seguro...?'
And if user confirms deletion
Then page sends DELETE /api/users/{id}
And user is removed from in-memory storage
And table reloads without deleted user
acceptance_criteria: confirm() dialog appears before DELETE request
DELETE /api/users/{id} is sent only if user confirms
API removes user from users array
Response status 200: {success: true, message: '...', user: {...}}
Response status 404 if user not found
Table automatically reloads after deletion

requirements_normalized.title: Cancel edit operation
requirements_normalized.gherkin:
Given edit modal is open
When user clicks 'Cancelar' button
Or clicks close button (X)
Or clicks outside modal
Then modal closes
And no changes are persisted
acceptance_criteria: Close button (X) closes modal without PUT request
Cancel button closes modal without PUT request
Clicking outside modal closes it (if enabled)
Modal displays: display='none'

requirements_normalized.title: Navigate to registration page
requirements_normalized.gherkin:
Given user is on any page
When user clicks 'Registro' link in navigation
Then page navigates to /
And index.html is served with registration form
acceptance_criteria: Navigation link href='/'
Page serves index.html on GET /
Page contains registration form with id='registrationForm'

requirements_normalized.title: Navigate to users listing page
requirements_normalized.gherkin:
Given user is on any page
When user clicks 'Ver Usuarios' link in navigation
Then page navigates to /users
And users.html is served with users table
acceptance_criteria: Navigation link href='/users'
Page serves users.html on GET /users
Page contains users table and loadUsers() is called

requirements_normalized.title: POST /api/register returns correct HTTP status and format
acceptance_criteria: Status 200 on valid registration
Response: {success: true, message: 'User registered successfully', user: {...}}
User ID is numeric (Date.now())
registeredAt is timestamp in locale format

requirements_normalized.title: API error responses for validation failures
requirements_normalized.gherkin:
Given invalid registration payload
When name is missing
Or email is missing
Or both are missing
Then API responds with status 400
And response body: {error: 'Name and email are required'}
acceptance_criteria: Missing name: 400 with error message
Missing email: 400 with error message
Invalid JSON: 400 with error message
Malformed request body: 400 with error message

requirements_normalized.title: GET /api/users returns array and handles empty state
requirements_normalized.gherkin:
Given any state of users in memory
When GET /api/users is called
Then API responds with status 200
And response Content-Type is 'application/json'
And response body is array (possibly empty)
And each element has structure: {id, name, email, phone, registeredAt}
acceptance_criteria: Status 200 always
Returns JSON array []
On empty: returns []
On populated: each user has all fields

requirements_normalized.title: GET /api/users/:id returns single user or 404
requirements_normalized.gherkin:
Given a user with id exists
When GET /api/users/{id} is called
Then API responds with status 200
And response body is user object
Given no user with id exists
When GET /api/users/{id} is called
Then API responds with status 404
And response body: {error: 'User not found'}
acceptance_criteria: Status 200 for existing user
Status 404 for non-existent user
404 response: {error: 'User not found'}

gaps_identified.title: Email validation beyond HTML5
gaps_identified.description: API does not validate email format at backend. Only HTML5 email type on frontend. No RFC 5322 validation, no domain existence check, no duplicate email prevention.
gaps_identified.impact: Registration suite cannot fully test email validation. Risk of invalid emails being stored. Security risk for email enumeration.
gaps_identified.recommendation: Planner should design tests assuming basic HTML5 validation only. Flag for dev team: implement email format + duplicate check.

gaps_identified.title: No rate limiting on API endpoints
gaps_identified.description: No rate limiting implemented on POST /api/register or other endpoints. Could allow abuse (registration spam, deletion loops).
gaps_identified.impact: Cannot test rate limiting. Security suite cannot verify DDoS protection. Load testing scope unclear.
gaps_identified.recommendation: Planner should scope out rate limiting tests as out-of-scope. Flag for dev team: implement rate limiting before production.

gaps_identified.title: In-memory storage with no persistence
gaps_identified.description: All user data stored in memory (let users = []). Data is lost when server restarts. No database backend, no file storage, no session persistence.
gaps_identified.impact: Cannot test data persistence or recovery. Test suite cannot span server restarts. Each test run starts with empty user list.
gaps_identified.recommendation: Planner should design tests assuming stateless server. Use setup/teardown to ensure known state. Flag for dev team: implement persistent storage for production.

gaps_identified.title: No authentication or authorization
gaps_identified.description: No user login, no session management, no role-based access control. Any user can access, modify, delete any user record.
gaps_identified.impact: Security testing scope undefined. User isolation tests not applicable. Access control tests cannot be designed.
gaps_identified.recommendation: Planner should exclude auth tests from scope. Flag for dev team: implement authentication before multi-user scenarios.

gaps_identified.title: Limited error messages and status codes
gaps_identified.description: Only 200, 400, 404 status codes used. No 500 error scenario documented. Error messages are generic ({error: 'Invalid request'}).
gaps_identified.impact: API contract tests limited to basic happy path and validation errors. Cannot test server error handling.
gaps_identified.recommendation: Planner should design tests for 200/400/404 cases. Flag for dev team: add error scenarios (500, 503, etc.).

gaps_identified.title: No field length limits
gaps_identified.description: API accepts name, email, phone of any length. HTML5 has no maxlength attributes. No backend length validation.
gaps_identified.impact: Boundary value testing not applicable. Cannot test buffer overflow or storage limits.
gaps_identified.recommendation: Planner should design tests assuming no length limits. Flag for dev team: add maxlength to fields and backend validation.

gaps_identified.title: No XSS sanitization visible
gaps_identified.description: Form inputs are rendered directly into HTML (innerHTML in users-script.js line 38). No HTML escaping or sanitization visible in backend responses.
gaps_identified.impact: Security testing should include XSS injection scenarios. Current implementation may be vulnerable.
gaps_identified.recommendation: Planner should include XSS attack vectors in security suite. Flag for dev team: implement input sanitization and output encoding.

gaps_identified.title: Phone field validation undefined
gaps_identified.description: Phone field is optional but has type='tel' on frontend only. Backend accepts any string, no format validation.
gaps_identified.impact: Unclear what valid phone formats are. International vs local? Numbers only? Symbols?
gaps_identified.recommendation: Planner should test with various phone formats and document behavior. Flag for dev team: clarify phone format requirements.

gaps_identified.title: No pagination on user listing
gaps_identified.description: GET /api/users returns all users without pagination, sorting, or filtering. Returns entire array.
gaps_identified.impact: Performance testing with large datasets not applicable. UI rendering performance limits unknown.
gaps_identified.recommendation: Planner should test with reasonable data sizes (< 1000 users). Flag for dev team: implement pagination for scalability.

gaps_identified.title: No soft delete or audit trail
gaps_identified.description: DELETE endpoint removes users permanently. No audit log, no soft delete, no restoration capability.
gaps_identified.impact: Cannot test data recovery or audit compliance.
gaps_identified.recommendation: Planner should scope out recovery/audit tests. Flag for dev team: implement soft delete for production use.

gaps_identified.title: HTML5 validation assumptions
gaps_identified.description: Frontend relies on HTML5 email type, tel type, required attribute. No fallback for older browsers.
gaps_identified.recommendation: Planner should test on modern browsers (Chrome 90+, Firefox 88+, Safari 14+). Older browser testing out-of-scope.

gaps_identified.title: User ID generation strategy not documented
gaps_identified.description: User ID is Date.now() (milliseconds since epoch). Not explicitly documented. Could have collisions in high-concurrency scenarios.
gaps_identified.impact: ID format assumptions in test data. Collision risk at scale.
gaps_identified.recommendation: Planner should use unique timestamps or UUIDs for test data. Flag for dev team: document ID strategy or use UUIDs.

must_validate: Revisar los 12 gaps y determinar cuáles impiden diseño de test suites (CRITICAL vs advisories)
Validar que 18 requisitos son suficientes para planificar cobertura realista (80%+ coverage posible?)
Verificar que las 4 áreas (Registration, Listing, Management, Navigation) son suficientes como particiones o si se necesitan más (API contracts como área separada?)
Confirmar con dev team sobre persistencia y rate limiting antes de escalar a load tests
can_skip: No necesitas re-extraer requisitos; están todos y normalizados en Gherkin
No necesitas re-verificar trazabilidad; cada requisito apunta a líneas de código específicas
No necesitas implementar los gaps; solo documentarlos para planificación
decision_points: ¿Cómo modelar cobertura de Registration suite sin validación de email backend? ¿Asumir HTML5 solo?
¿Necesita Listing suite stress tests? (GAP-009: sin pagination - ¿cuántos users son 'realistas'?)
¿Incluir XSS injection tests? (GAP-007: posible vulnerabilidad detectada)
¿La suite de Management (Edit/Delete) necesita precondiciones de autenticación? (GAP-004: sin auth implementada)
¿Cómo tratar GAP-003 (no persistencia)? ¿Scope en-memory tests solo o flag para próxima iteración?
¿Priorizar identificar y automatizar qué? (18 requisitos → cuáles son must-test vs nice-to-have?)

if_gaps_found.note: Si Planner encuentra gaps adicionales durante diseño de suites que no están en esta lista

if_coverage_impossible.note: Si los 18 requisitos + gaps no permiten diseñar cobertura mínima aceptable (< 50%)

if_conflict_detected.conflict_resolution_strategy: Si hay conflictos en requisitos (ej: email validation rules contradictorias), contactar test_documentation para clarificación o dev team para resolverlos

api_endpoints_documented.description: Get a specific user by ID
